worth_it_explanation: Broken click-through URLs, silent third-party tags, and creatives stuck in policy review can each spend budget without delivering value. A creative with a dead landing page burns impressions. A tag that never fires leaves verification and attribution dark. A disapproved creative that the platform serves anyway, or withholds without warning, disrupts delivery silently. These three checks take 30-60 minutes across a flight and protect the work done in every other part of the account.
intro: Display and Video 360 (DV360) separates creative review into two parallel tracks: a landing page and creative review and a content and policy review. A creative can pass the first and stall on the second, or clear both and still fail to deliver at the exchange level. Third-party impression, click, and verification tags are wired manually to each creative and receive no automatic validation inside the platform. These checks confirm that the click-through destinations are live and correct, that all tracking tags are present and firing, and that no creative is sitting in a policy disapproval state that blocks delivery without an obvious alert.

audit_checks.title: Click-through URLs on creatives are dead, redirecting to wrong destinations, or mismatched to the line item's targeting
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Every impression delivered against a broken or wrong destination wastes the media spend behind it. The click still counts, the budget still depletes, and the conversion never fires. Attribution shows zero return on real spend.

educate.base:
In Display and Video 360, the click-through URL lives inside the creative's exit event, which the platform calls a click tag. The exit event carries the URL that opens when a user clicks the ad. Every creative must have at least one exit event, and that URL must resolve to a live, correct page at the time the impression runs, not only at the time the creative was uploaded.

A click-through URL breaks for several reasons. The destination page is taken down after launch. A campaign redirector chain fails partway through. A creative built for one product is repurposed for a new flight without updating the destination. A seasonal landing page expires while the line item continues to run. In each case, the creative serves, the budget depletes, and the click resolves to a 404 or to the site's homepage rather than the intended product or offer page.

The mismatch problem is different from the broken-URL problem. A URL can return a 200 status and still be wrong, for example pointing to a generic brand page when the line item targets users searching for a specific product, or routing desktop users and mobile users to destinations that differ in ways that affect conversion rate.

A few signs suggest this check applies:
  • Click-through rate is within normal range but the downstream conversion rate from clicks is far below benchmark
  • The creative has been active for more than two weeks since a site or campaign refresh
  • The creative was duplicated from a prior flight without a destination review
You may want to click through every active creative variant during a review, not just the primary. Backup exit events and multi-exit rich media units can each carry a different URL.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: On a store, the landing page should match the product or category the creative promotes. A click-through URL that lands on a homepage rather than the promoted product page adds friction that the impression spend cannot recover. You may want to confirm each creative's destination matches the offer in the ad copy, and that the page is in stock and purchasable rather than showing an out-of-stock or removed-product state.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: On a lead-generation site, the destination page should carry the form or offer the creative references. You may want to confirm that the page loads fully on mobile. DV360 campaigns frequently weight mobile inventory, and that any gated content the creative references is accessible without an unexpected paywall or login redirect.

learn_more.source_title: ExitEvent — click-through URL structure for DV360 creatives

investigate.intro: Confirm that each active creative's click-through URL resolves to the intended, live destination.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the advertiser and navigate to 'Creative'. Filter to 'Active' status to focus on creatives currently running or eligible to run.

For each creative, open the creative detail and find the exit event URL. Copy the URL and paste it into a browser to confirm it resolves to the correct destination and returns a live page rather than a 404, redirect loop, or generic homepage.
For HTML5 or rich media creatives with multiple exit events, preview the creative and click each tap area to confirm each exit resolves correctly.
Note any URL that lands on a page unrelated to the creative's offer, a page that has been removed or replaced, or a page that redirects to an unexpected destination.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active creative, whether the click-through URL resolves to the correct live destination. A creative whose primary exit event URL returns a live, correct page passes. A creative whose URL returns a 404, a redirect to an unrelated page, or a page that does not match the offer in the creative is a fail recorded with the creative name and the destination observed.
evaluate.fallback_message: Click through each active creative's exit event URL and record the destination above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more active creatives on {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 campaigns have click-through URLs pointing to dead or incorrect destinations. Impressions served against these creatives deplete budget without producing clicks that convert, and attribution shows no return on the affected spend. {{voice_recommend}} auditing every active creative's exit event URL, replacing any URL that does not resolve to the correct live page, and scheduling a destination review as part of any flight that runs more than two weeks or reuses creatives from a prior campaign.

audit_checks.title: Third-party impression, click, or verification tags are missing from creatives or are not firing
audit_checks.impact_rationale: Missing or silent third-party tags leave impression counts, click verification, and brand-safety signals dark. Reporting from the verification vendor shows no data or partial data, and any discrepancy between DV360 and the ad server cannot be investigated without a tag that fires on both sides.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 supports third-party tracking URLs on creatives through two mechanisms. For video creatives, the platform exposes a ThirdPartyUrl field that accepts tracking pixels for events including impressions, clicks, first-quartile, midpoint, third-quartile, and completion. For display creatives, third-party tags are embedded directly in the creative HTML or passed as additional impression trackers on the placement.

Verification vendors such as Integral Ad Science (IAS) and DoubleVerify (DV) deliver their brand-safety, viewability, and invalid-traffic signals through these tags. A campaign configured to measure with a third-party vendor but missing the vendor's tag on one or more creatives produces a data gap that cannot be recovered retroactively. The vendor's reporting will show fewer impressions than DV360 reports, and the advertiser cannot determine whether those impressions met viewability or brand-safety thresholds.

Tags can be absent for several reasons. A creative was added after the initial trafficking setup. A template or creative format does not support a specific tag type. A tag was removed during a creative revision. The third-party verification campaign was configured in the vendor's platform but the tag was never copied into DV360.

A few signs suggest this check applies:
  • The verification vendor's reporting covers fewer impressions than DV360 reports for the same line item and date range
  • Some creatives were added mid-flight or updated after launch
  • The DV360 creative detail shows no third-party tracking URLs on a creative that the verification contract covers
You may want to cross-reference the number of creatives in the flight against the number of creatives the vendor's platform shows receiving data before concluding tags are complete.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, viewability data from the verification vendor informs how creative size and placement affect the likelihood of a product being seen before a purchase decision. Missing tags on lower-funnel retargeting creatives, which are the ones most directly tied to conversion, reduces the ability to connect viewability to return on ad spend.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, brand-safety and invalid-traffic data from the verification vendor protects the lead quality signals that feed into bidding. A missing tag on a creative served in high-volume display placements can allow invalid traffic to pollute the conversion data that Smart Bidding learns from.

learn_more.source_title: ThirdPartyUrl — tracking URL types for DV360 video creatives

investigate.intro: Confirm that every active creative in the flight carries the required third-party tracking tags and that the vendor's reporting reflects the expected impression volume.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the advertiser and navigate to 'Creative'. For each active video creative, open the creative detail and find the 'Third-party tracking URLs' section. Confirm that impression and click tracking URLs from the verification vendor are present for the required event types.
For display creatives, open the creative HTML or review the trafficking sheet and confirm the vendor's impression pixel is embedded.
Note any creative that has no third-party tracking URLs, or whose tracking URLs cover fewer event types than the verification contract requires.

verification_vendor_dashboard.label: Verification vendor dashboard (IAS or DoubleVerify)
steps: Open the verification vendor's dashboard and navigate to the campaign or placement report that matches the DV360 flight.

Compare the vendor's total measured impressions against DV360's total impressions for the same line item and date range. A material gap, typically more than 5%, suggests one or more creatives are not tagged.
If the vendor's platform shows creative-level data, identify which creatives show zero measured impressions while DV360 shows delivery. Those are the creatives with missing tags.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active creative in the flight, whether the required third-party tracking tags are present and whether the verification vendor's reporting covers the expected impression volume. A creative with all required tags present and vendor impressions within 5% of DV360 impressions passes. A creative with missing tags or a vendor impression gap greater than 5% is a fail recorded with the creative name and the missing tag type.
evaluate.fallback_message: Check each active creative for third-party tracking URLs and compare vendor impressions to DV360 impressions above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more creatives in {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 flights are missing third-party impression, click, or verification tags, or that the verification vendor's measured impressions fall materially below DV360's reported delivery. This gap leaves viewability, brand-safety, and invalid-traffic data incomplete for the affected creatives, and any discrepancy between DV360 and the ad server cannot be investigated without a tag firing on both sides. {{voice_recommend}} auditing every active creative for the required third-party tracking URLs, adding missing tags for the affected creatives, and cross-referencing the vendor's impression count against DV360 after a 48-hour window to confirm coverage is complete.

audit_checks.title: Creatives are in a policy disapproval state that blocks or restricts delivery without a visible alert
audit_checks.impact_rationale: A creative in APPROVAL_STATUS_REJECTED_NOT_SERVABLE or APPROVAL_STATUS_PENDING_NOT_SERVABLE does not serve, but the line item continues to pace against its budget goal using other creatives or simply under-delivers. Disapproval is not always surfaced in the campaign view, so spend can fall short of plan without a clear reason.

educate.base:
Display and Video 360 runs creatives through two sequential review tracks before they are eligible to serve. The first track is a landing page and creative review that checks whether the ad and its destination meet basic technical and policy requirements. The second is a content and policy review that evaluates the creative against Google's advertising policies. The ReviewStatus object reports four possible states: approved and servable, pending but servable (passed the first track, awaiting the second), pending and not servable, or rejected and not servable.

A rejected creative does not serve, but DV360 does not always surface the disapproval prominently in the campaign or insertion order (IO) view. If the line item has other approved creatives, it continues to deliver. The rejected creative simply carries no impressions, and the line item may under-deliver against its budget or its reach goal depending on how many approved variants remain.

Disapprovals happen for several reasons. A creative references a restricted product category such as financial services or health and pharmaceuticals. An image creative includes text that exceeds the 20% text guideline. A landing page changed after the creative was approved, and a re-review found a policy issue on the new page. An ad network at the exchange level applies its own policy and rejects the creative even after Google's review approves it.

A few signs suggest this check applies:
  • The line item is pacing below goal despite sufficient budget and bid
  • The creative library shows one or more creatives with a status other than 'Active' or 'Approved'
  • A new creative was uploaded in the past two weeks and has not yet served any impressions
You may want to check creative review status at the start of any flight and again after any creative update. An update can trigger a re-review that temporarily removes a previously approved creative from delivery.

site_type_overlays.ecommerce: For an ecommerce advertiser, a disapproval on a promotional creative during a sale window can lose the entire period the promotion was meant to cover. You may want to submit promotional creatives for review several days before the sale starts. Re-review after a fix can take 24-48 hours and is not guaranteed before the window closes.
site_type_overlays.lead_gen: For a lead-generation advertiser, disapprovals in regulated categories such as financial products, healthcare, or employment are more frequent. You may want to review Google's restricted content policies for the advertiser's vertical before trafficking and route the creative through a policy compliance check before uploading.

learn_more.source_title: ReviewStatus — creative approval and exchange review states in DV360

investigate.intro: Confirm that every creative associated with active line items is in an approved and servable state, and investigate any creative that is not.

dv360_ui.label: DV360 UI
steps: Open the advertiser and navigate to 'Creative'. Filter by status to surface creatives that are not 'Approved' or 'Active'. Note any creatives in a 'Disapproved', 'Pending', or 'Under review' state.

For each non-approved creative, open its detail view and read the review status message. Identify which policy or content requirement triggered the disapproval and whether the creative is associated with an active line item.
Cross-reference disapproved creatives against the insertion order and line item that should be serving them. If the line item has no other approved creative to fall back on, it is not delivering.
If the disapproval cites a policy issue on the landing page rather than the ad itself, confirm that the landing page currently live matches the page that was reviewed.

evaluate.logic: The practitioner records, per active line item, whether all associated creatives are in an approved and servable review state. A line item whose associated creatives are all approved and servable passes. A line item with one or more creatives in a rejected or non-servable pending state, particularly where those creatives are the only variant or where the line item is under-delivering, is a fail recorded with the creative name, the disapproval reason, and the affected line item.
evaluate.fallback_message: Review the approval status of each creative associated with active line items above to see the evaluation.

generate.narrative_template: {{voice_found}} that one or more creatives associated with active line items on {{site_name}}'s Display and Video 360 campaigns are in a policy disapproval or non-servable pending state, which prevents them from delivering. The affected line items may be under-pacing against their budget goals or relying on fewer creative variants than planned. {{voice_recommend}} reviewing the disapproval reason for each affected creative in the DV360 creative detail view, correcting the policy issue or landing page problem the review identified, and resubmitting for review. For campaigns in regulated categories, {{voice_recommend}} building a review buffer of at least three business days into the creative production schedule so disapprovals can be resolved before the flight goes live.
